channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CDC investigates Cyclospora outbreak linked to Taco Bell lettuce supply chain
item.description: Federal health officials are investigating whether shredded iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms in California was the source of a nationwide Cyclospora outbreak affecting over 1,600 people across 34 sta...

item.title: Cyclosporiasis Outbreak Sparks Widespread Anxiety and Social Media Concerns
item.description: A nationwide outbreak of cyclosporiasis, a parasitic infection causing explosive diarrhea, has triggered significant anxiety across the U.S., amplified by social media platforms. The Centers for Disea...

item.title: Nile Media Entertainment Group, Idris Elba's 22Summers and Action Xtreme Launch African Action Film Partnership
item.description: Moses Babatope’s Nile Media Entertainment Group, Chee Keong Cheung’s Action Xtreme, and Idris Elba’s 22Summers have formed a strategic partnership to produce action films rooted in West African storie...

item.title: Edmonton Elks secure win with strong defense and Blanchard's field goals
item.description: The Edmonton Elks extended their winning streak with a 19-17 victory over the B.C. Lions, showcasing a dominant defensive performance and reliable kicking from Vincent Blanchard. The game, played at E...

item.title: Fattah's Visit Promotes Tanzania-Egypt Relations and Infrastructure Projects
item.description: The article highlights President Samia Suluhu Hassan's recent visit to Egypt, aimed at strengthening bilateral ties and exploring opportunities for collaboration. During the visit, discussions focused...

item.title: Pakistan and Kuwait Explore Expanded Defence Pact Amid US-Iran Tensions
item.description: Pakistan and Kuwait are in early-stage negotiations for an expanded defence pact, with Kuwait seeking troop deployments, fighter jets, and drones in exchange for energy cooperation. Sources indicate t...

item.title: Tennessee Woman Arrested for Alleged Attempt to Drown Child in Backyard Pool
item.description: A 31-year-old Tennessee woman, Mikaylee Rae Beirne, was arrested after authorities alleged she attempted to drown one of her children in a backyard swimming pool. The incident occurred on July 15, 202...

item.title: Fraudster jailed for £11.5m scam targeting elderly in Suffolk
item.description: Steven Long, 59, from Kesgrave, was sentenced to eight years and four months in prison for orchestrating a £11.5m fraud scheme targeting elderly victims in Suffolk and surrounding areas. The scam, ope...

item.title: India's Private Rocket Vikram-1 Successfully Launches into Space, Marks Milestone in Commercial Space Sector
item.description: India's first privately developed orbital rocket, Vikram-1, successfully launched from Sriharikota on Saturday, marking a significant milestone for the country's commercial space ambitions. Developed ...

item.title: Trump threatens Canada with tariffs over wildfire smoke impacting US air quality
item.description: Donald Trump has accused Canada of 'Willful Negligence' in forest management, threatening additional tariffs due to wildfire smoke that has severely impacted air quality across the United States. Over...

item.title: South Africa Alone Gets Visa-Free Access to Russia, Others Face Restrictions
item.description: Russia has outlined visa-free travel conditions for select African countries, with South Africa being the only nation allowing visa-free entry for all passport holders. Citizens from 16 other African ...

item.title: NGO Advocates Enhanced Safeguards for Domestic Workers
item.description: The article highlights a call by a non-governmental organization (NGO) for improved legal and social protections for domestic workers in Tanzania. The NGO emphasizes the need for stricter regulations ...

item.title: Dune: Part Three Concludes the Trilogy with Emotional Farewell
item.description: The article discusses the final chapter of the *Dune* film trilogy, *Dune: Part Three*, directed by Denis Villeneuve. It highlights the emotional closure for the cast, including Timothée Chalamet, who...

item.title: Russia's Diesel Ban and Geopolitical Tensions Impact Mining Transport Costs in South Africa
item.description: The article discusses how Russia's diesel export ban, triggered by Ukrainian drone attacks on its refining infrastructure, has disrupted global diesel markets. This has led to soaring diesel prices, s...

item.title: LTFRB Imposes P60 Cap on App-Based Taxi Booking Fees
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has implemented a new regulation limiting the booking and platform fees for app-based taxis to a maximum of P60. This measure, outlined...

item.title: Challenges for Apartment Dwellers in Adopting Rooftop Solar in Australia
item.description: The article highlights the disparity in solar adoption between standalone homes and apartment dwellers in Australia. Research by Energy Consumers Australia reveals that 39% of homeowners have solar, c...

item.title: Messi Comments on Viral Photo with Lamine Yamal Ahead of World Cup Final
item.description: Lionel Messi described the viral photo of himself holding baby Lamine Yamal as 'crazy' during a press conference ahead of their World Cup final matchup. The image, which shows Messi cradling the infan...

item.title: Tanzania Insight News Update: July 18, 2026
item.description: On July 18, 2026, Tanzania Insight reported on two key events. First, Tanzanian President Fattah's visit to Egypt aimed to strengthen bilateral relations, focusing on trade, investment, and regional c...

item.title: World Cup Sparks Growth in Soccer Participation Across Northern Ontario
item.description: The article highlights how the World Cup has ignited renewed interest in soccer among youth in northern Ontario, leading to increased participation and local initiatives. While the main story focuses ...

item.title: Omtatah Calls for Clarity on 3-Year Delay in KEMRI Leadership Appointment
item.description: Busia Senator Okiya Omtatah has raised concerns about the prolonged vacancy at Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI), which has operated without a substantive director-general since 2022. In a stat...
